questions.text: Ohm's law is valid only for currents flowing in
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Semiconductor devices capable of amplifying or switching electronic signals.
Discharges of electricity that occur when a current flows through a gap in a medium, producing a luminous plasma.
Ohm's law is valid only for currents flowing in metallic conductors.
Semiconductor devices that allow current to flow in one direction only, commonly used in rectification and signal demodulation.

questions.explanation: Ohm's law is valid only for currents flowing in metallic conductors.

questions.text: The unit of magnetic flux density is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The unit of magnetic flux density is the Tesla (T), named after the inventor Nikola Tesla.
"Wb/m²" is derived from the SI unit of magnetic flux (Weber, Wb) divided by the area (square meters).
"N·A^-1·m^-1" is another way of expressing magnetic flux density, where a Newton per ampere per meter is equivalent to a Tesla (T), the SI unit of magnetic flux density.
The unit of magnetic flux density are Tesla (T), "Wb/m²" (Weber per square meter) and "N·A^-1·m^-1" (Newton per ampere per meter)

questions.explanation: The unit of magnetic flux density are Tesla (T), "Wb/m²" (Weber per square meter) and "N·A^-1·m^-1" (Newton per ampere per meter)

questions.text: curve is graph between
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This graph is commonly used to represent voltage waveforms over time in electrical circuits, such as alternating current (AC) waveforms or transient voltage spikes.
A curve is typically a graph between:Voltage and TimeVoltage and CurrentCurrent and Time
This graph illustrates the relationship between voltage and current in a component or circuit. It can show characteristics like Ohm's law in resistors or the voltage-current relationship in diodes.
Similar to the voltage-time graph, this graph represents current waveforms over time in electrical circuits, such as in AC circuits or transient current behavior during circuit switching.

questions.explanation: A curve is typically a graph between:Voltage and TimeVoltage and CurrentCurrent and Time
